name: Oats

identity.basisReason: Dry per-100g basis matches how packaged oats are labeled and avoids cooked-water dilution.

metricProvenance.header: USDA FoodData Central style raw dry rolled-oats entry, normalized to per 100 g edible portion
metricProvenance.saturated_fat_g: same canonical source family as header
metricProvenance.omega3_mg: derived from ALA value when present in source family; acceptable but not a ranking-critical metric here
metricProvenance.polyunsaturated_fat_g: same canonical source family as header
metricProvenance.cholesterol_mg: plant food default, canonical expectation confirmed by source family
metricProvenance.starch_g: same canonical source family as header
metricProvenance.fibre_g: same canonical source family as header
metricProvenance.sugar_g: same canonical source family as header
metricProvenance.glycemic_index: withheld, too preparation-dependent to treat as a locked canonical fact for dry rolled oats
metricProvenance.essential_amino_acids_score: withheld, proxy score not yet backed by a locked amino-acid source table for this exact food form
metricProvenance.nonessential_amino_acids_score: withheld, proxy score not yet backed by a locked amino-acid source table for this exact food form
metricProvenance.bioavailability_percent: withheld, proxy estimate not production-safe for this plant food entry
metricProvenance.vitamin_a_dv: same canonical source family as header
metricProvenance.vitamin_c_dv: same canonical source family as header
metricProvenance.vitamin_d_dv: same canonical source family as header

pros.title: contains meaningful beta-glucan soluble fibre
pros.explanation: Oats are unusual among common grains for their beta-glucan content, which makes the fibre story more specific than just the fibre grams already shown.

pros.title: easy whole-grain staple to use regularly
pros.explanation: Plain rolled oats are cheap, familiar, and realistic for repeated use, which matters for an everyday grain.

pros.title: plain oats start unsweetened
pros.explanation: That gives them a cleaner baseline than sweetened instant oatmeal products, which are excluded from this entry.

cons.title: phytates can modestly reduce mineral uptake
cons.explanation: That caveat is real for oats, even if it does not erase their mineral content.

cons.title: the bowl can get worse fast with sugary add-ons
cons.explanation: The plain ingredient is solid, but common preparation choices can change the real meal a lot.

cons.title: texture and tolerance are not universal wins
cons.explanation: Some people dislike the texture or do not tolerate oats especially well, which is a real usability downside for a staple food.

scoreReadiness.summary: Identity, basis, macros, fibre, starch, and core micronutrients are solid enough to use. Grain scoring still needs a locked GI policy for dry oats versus prepared oats.
blockers: glycemic_index intentionally withheld because it is preparation-dependent
grain protein proxy metrics were removed until exact-source amino acid data is attached

sourceNotes: Identity locked to plain rolled oats, dry, per 100 g as sold.
Core nutrient values align with common USDA FoodData Central style entries for dry oats/rolled oats.
Weak proxy metrics were removed rather than preserved as fake certainty.
